item.ptxt: With OBD system of type A, DTC with in the following table cannot be read with CAN communication OBD-II generic scan tool.
For details of D/C (driving cycle), refer to OBD System Description .
There are three types of OBD system depending on the vehicle specification. For identification, refer to OBD System Description .
*1: MIL operation is applicable to type A only.
*2: With type A, light lights up.
*3: With type B, DTC is detected during 1 D/C.
*4: DTC with *4 is detected only for type B.
*5: DTC with *5 is detected only for type A and C.

entry.ptxt: Brake light switch signal is not received during 1 D/C even if driving at specified speed and for specified time.
Two or more transmission range sensor signals are input simultaneously for specified time.
Transmission range signal is not input for specified time.
Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor “A” Circuit Range / Performance
This DTC is detected if any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: CVT fluid temperature variation is lower than specified value even if engine is running for specified time after engine start.
CVT fluid temperature is higher than specified value even if engine running condition is lower than specified time.

entry.ptxt: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor “A” Circuit Low
CVT fluid temperature signal circuit voltage is lower than specified value for specified time.
Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor “A” Circuit High
CVT fluid temperature signal circuit voltage is higher than specified value for specified time.
Input/Turbine Speed Sensor “A” Circuit No Signal
This DTC is detected if any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Primary pulley speed signal is lower than specified value even if secondary pulley speed signal is higher than specified value.
Output signal value of primary pulley speed sensor is lower than half of the actual primary pulley speed for specified time.

entry.ptxt: Output Speed Sensor Circuit No Signal
This DTC is detected if any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Vehicle speed signal is lower than specified value even if secondary pulley speed signal is higher than specified value.
Output signal value of vehicle speed sensor is lower than half of the actual vehicle speed for specified time.

entry.ptxt: Torque Converter Clutch Circuit Performance / Stuck Off
Slip revolution for torque converter, which is calculated from primary pulley speed and engine speed, is more than specified value even if TCC solenoid valve is ON.
Intermediate Shaft Speed Sensor “A” Circuit No Signal
This DTC is detected if any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Secondary pulley speed signal is lower than specified value even if primary pulley speed signal is higher than specified value.
Output signal value of secondary pulley speed sensor is lower than half of the actual secondary pulley speed for specified time.

entry.ptxt: Pressure Control Solenoid “C” Performance / Stuck Off
Calculated ratio for planetary gear unit is more than specified value for specified time even if low brake solenoid is ON.
This DTC is detected if any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: When actual ratio of planetary gear unit is out of specification, deceleration ratio is higher than specification.
Planetary gear unit does not change to the 2nd speed for specified time, even if low brake solenoid is OFF.

entry.ptxt: Transmission Fluid Pressure Sensor / Switch “A” Circuit Range / Performance
Difference between actual line pressure and target line pressure is higher than specification when driving at a constant speed.
Transmission Fluid Pressure Sensor / Switch “A” Circuit Low
Secondary pressure sensor signal circuit voltage is lower than specified value for specified time.
Transmission Fluid Pressure Sensor / Switch “A” Circuit High
Secondary pressure sensor signal circuit voltage is higher than specified value for specified time.
Pressure Control Solenoid “A” Control Circuit Range / Performance
Gear ratio calculated from primary pulley speed sensor and secondary pulley speed sensor is more than specified value for specified time.
Pressure Control Solenoid “A” Control Circuit Low

Measured circuit current of line pressure control solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to ground, even if command circuit current for line pressure control solenoid valve is within specification.
Pressure Control Solenoid “A” Control Circuit High

Measured circuit current of line pressure control solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to power supply or open circuit, even if command circuit current for line pressure control solenoid valve is within specification.
Pressure Control Solenoid “B” Control Circuit Range / Performance
Pulley ratio is out of specification for specified time.
Pressure Control Solenoid “B” Control Circuit Low

Measured circuit current of primary pressure control solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to ground, even if command circuit current for primary pressure control solenoid valve is within specification.
Pressure Control Solenoid “B” Control Circuit High

Measured circuit current of primary pressure control solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to power supply or open circuit, even if command circuit current for primary pressure control solenoid valve is within specification.
Pressure Control Solenoid “C” Control Circuit Low
Measured circuit current of low brake control solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to ground, even if command circuit current for low brake control solenoid valve is within specification.
Pressure Control Solenoid “C” Control Circuit High

Measured circuit current of low brake control solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to power supply or open circuit, even if command circuit current for low brake control solenoid valve is within specification.
Internal Control Module Memory Check Sum Error
Invalid Data Received From ABS / ESP® Control Module
ESP® Control Module detects abnormality of wheel speed data.
Internal Control Module FLASH Memory Error
Torque Request Communication Error from TCM
ECM detects abnormality of CAN communication data which TCM transmits.
Slope sensor initialization is not completed.
TCM power supply voltage from IG1 relay is lower than specified value for specified time.
TCM power supply voltage from CVT relay is lower than specified value for specified time.
Primary pulley, secondary pulley and vehicle speed sensor signal is lower than specified value, even if engine speed is higher than specified value.
TCM and learning value in ROM disagree.
Lost Communication With Valve Body Assembly
TCM cannot read learning value in ROM.
Slope sensor signal voltage is lower than limit or higher than limit for specified time.
Slope sensor signal voltage and change in vehicle acceleration and deceleration do not match.
Pressure Control Solenoid “D” Performance / Stuck Off
This DTC is detected if any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Deceleration value is higher than specified value while actual ratio for planetary gear unit is out of specification.
Planetary gear unit does not shift to the 1st gear for specified time, even if high clutch & reverse brake control solenoid valve is ON.

entry.ptxt: Calculated ratio for planetary gear unit is more than specified value for specified time, even if high clutch & reverse brake control solenoid valve is OFF.
Pressure Control Solenoid “D” Control Circuit Low

Measured circuit current for high clutch & reverse brake control solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to ground, even if command circuit current for high clutch & reverse brake control solenoid valve is within specification.
Pressure Control Solenoid “D” Control Circuit High

Measured circuit current for high clutch & reverse brake control solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to power supply or open circuit, even if command circuit current for high clutch & reverse brake control solenoid valve is within specification.
Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit High

Measured circuit current for TCC (Torque Converter Clutch) solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to power supply or open circuit, even if command circuit current for TCC (Torque Converter Clutch) solenoid valve is within specification.
Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit Low

Measured circuit current for TCC (Torque Converter Clutch) solenoid valve is lower than limit due to short to ground, even if command circuit current for TCC (Torque Converter Clutch) solenoid valve is within specification.
Refer to CAN DTC (Lost Communication and Communication Bus Off) Table .
Lost Communication With ABS / ESP® Control Module
Lost Communication With Body Control Module
Lost Communication With Instrument Panel Cluster (IPC) Control Module
